titleStmt.title: SII 17.563: original edition by K. G. Krishnan
No. 563. (A.R. No. 520 of 1904.) TIRUNELLIKKĀVAL, MANNARGUDI TALUK, TANJORE DISTRICT.

summary.p: On the north base of the central shrine in the Āmalakēśvara temple.
On palaeographical considerations this inscription can be assigned to Rājarāja III.

It records a grant of land, made tax-free by the sthānattār of the temple for burning a lamp at the Kulachchiṟai maThaThe maTha and the guhai had been evidently named after the Pāṇḍya minister Kulachchiṟai. and the saint TiruñAṉasambandhar, both of whom lived in about the 7th century A.D.situated in the tirumaḍaiviḷAgam to the north of the temple of Tirunellikkā-uḍaiyār. It mentions TiruñAṉasambandan guhai,The maTha and the guhai had been evidently named after the Pāṇḍya minister Kulachchiṟai. and the saint TiruñAṉasambandhar, both of whom lived in about the 7th century A.D.which was probably a monastery (See No. 564 below).

revisionDesc.change: Further conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)
Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
svastiśrī . tiripuvaṉacakkaravattikaḷ śrīrājarā[ja*]tevaṟku yāṇṭu 2 10 1 vatu Uṭaiyār tiru nellikkā Uṭaiyār koyil vaṭaciṟakiṟ tirumaṭaiviḷākattu kiḻtalaiyil kūttāṭu(m) māṇṭār Irukkiṟa kulacciṟai maṭam tiripuvanaviricirattu
1 can=ti viḷakku tarmamāka veṇum meṉṟu Ūrpotuvāna nilattu Iṭṭa rāna tiripuvanavirapurattu nātan
vatikku kiḻakku Uṭaiyār śrīkayilācamuṭaiyār tirumaṭaiviḷākattu tiruñānacampantan kukai kku talaimmāṟu viṭṭa nilattukku kiḻa
kkaṭaiyāy niṅka viṭṭu nāyanār Iṟaiyiliyāka Anupavittu varukiṟa viḷākamum kuḻi 2 10 5 Iviḷākanilam kuḻi Irupattañcum Iṟaiyiliyāka
kaikkoṇṭu māyeśvarapitiṭṭaiyāka viṭṭa Innāyanār tirumaṭaiviḷākattum muṉaikke kiḻakku ti lāna kol nūṟukolum Itan Uṭkol Ā kkalum Āka yka viṭṭa kulacciṟai maṭa [2 10 5]ikkuḻi Iru pattañcum
pitiṭṭaiyāka cantirātittavaraiyum Iṟaiyiliyāka kuṭuttom Ikkoyil tānattārom .
Digital edition of SII 17.563 by converted to DHARMA conventions by Emmanuel Francis.
